causalEvidence: Round 1 opposite-order ballots both chose screen-left, resolving to opposite jaw_taper candidates; both confidence values were 0.58.
Round 2 opposite-order ballots both chose screen-left, resolving to opposite head_width candidates; confidence values were 0.57 and 0.54.
Round 3 primary chose one cheek candidate at 0.54 while audit returned tie at 0.70.
No ballot pair met physical-candidate agreement plus confidence threshold, so root-owned verifier receipts never authorized promotion.
diagnosis: The current agent evaluator can describe large missing facial structures but is not reliable enough to rank the available subtle one-axis variations. Reversed ordering exposed position sensitivity. The search therefore cannot claim that the initial champion is optimal or close to the target.
